title: POMDP: Full Trajectory in the Tiny Corridor

runtime.expected_outcome: Agent reaches the goal in <=3 steps; every tick shows a clean perceive -> plan -> act cycle in the Glass Engine.

symbols.G(pi): expected free energy of the policy

audiences.kid: The agent has a tiny hallway and a goal at the end. It looks, thinks, moves, and arrives in three hops.
audiences.real: Minimal end-to-end POMDP demo: perceive the current tile, plan 2 steps ahead, act, repeat. Verifies the Markov-blanket-separated plumbing.
audiences.equation: pi* = softmax(-G). G decomposes into epistemic (information gain) and pragmatic (preference satisfaction) terms. Here the pragmatic term dominates.
audiences.derivation: Under the variational posterior q, expected free energy equals complexity minus expected log-preference. Softmax over -G gives the policy posterior (Friston et al. 2017).

orchestrate.objective: Run a full perceive-plan-act trajectory to the goal in the smallest maze.
orchestrate.role: End-to-end sanity check for the native Jido agent.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 4 & App B.
